title: ChroSpin - IMAC
author: Alexandra Ehl
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.tv3en8n

steps.instruction: Add 500 µl of nickel or cobalt solution, close the lid, and incubate 1 minute with end-over-end mixing. 00:01:00 Apart from Ni 2+ and Co 2+ , you can also use Cu 2+ or Zn 2+ . The affinity of histidine towards the metal ions is in the order Cu > Ni > Zn > Co. However, the specificity is in the invers order, i.e. copper will most likely result in best yields but with lower purity. In comparison, cobalt will result in a better purity but also lower yields.
Remove the bottom lid. Centrifuge the column at 1000 g for 30 - 60 seconds. Discard the flow through.
Add 500 µl deionized water, close the lid, invert sharply 3 - 5 times, and centrifuge the column at 1000 g for 30 - 60 seconds. Discard the flow through. Repeat once more.
Add 500 µl wash buffer, close the lid, invert sharply 3 - 5 times, and centrifuge the column at 1000 g for 30 - 60 seconds.

Place the column into a fresh collection tube. Add up to 500 µl cleared lysate, close the lid, and incubate 5 minutes with end-over-end mixing. Centrifuge the column at 1000 g for 30 - 60 seconds. Repeat the loading step when your sample volume is larger than 500 µl. This is particularly recommended when your target protein has low expression levels. It is advisable to keep the flow-through and wash fractions for later analysis, e.g. SDS-PAGE.

Place the column into a fresh collection tube. Add 500 µl wash buffer, close the lid, and invert sharply 3 - 5 times. Centrifuge the column at 1000 g for 30 - 60 seconds and empty the collection tube. Repeat once more without changing the collection tube. For increased purity, repeat the washing step up to 5 times.

Place the column into a fresh collection tube. Add 200 µl elution buffer to the column. Centrifuge the column at 1000 g for 30 - 60 seconds. Without changing the collection tube, repeat this elution step two more times.
How do you want to purify your protein? Do you want to prepare your column for reuse or sanitize it? Please choose below.

Wash Buffer 0.05 Molarity (M), Sodium dihydrogen phosphate 4 Molarity (M), Urea 0.3 Molarity (M), Sodium chloride Adjust to pH 8.0 with NaOH Elution Buffer 0.05 Molarity (M), Sodium dihydrogen phosphate 4 Molarity (M), Urea 0.3 Molarity (M), Sodium chloride 0.250 Molarity (M), Imidazole Adjust to pH 8.0 with NaOH Buffers containing urea are not stable at room temperature. Prepare freshly, use the buffers on the same day or store in appropriately sized aliquots at -20 °C for no longer than two months. Do not freeze and thaw the buffer more than once.

Add 500 µl of nickel or cobalt solution, close the lid, and incubate 1 minute with end-over-end mixing. 00:01:00 Apart from Ni 2+ and Co 2+ , you can also use Cu 2+ or Zn 2+ . The affinity of histidine towards the metal ions is in the order Cu > Ni > Zn > Co. However, the specificity is in the invers order, i.e. copper will most likely result in best yields but with lower purity. In comparison, cobalt will result in a better purity but also lower yields.
Remove the bottom lid. Centrifuge the column at 1000 g for 30 - 60 seconds. Discard the flow through.
Add 500 µl deionized water, close the lid, invert sharply 3 - 5 times, and centrifuge the column at 1000 g for 30 - 60 seconds. Discard the flow through. Repeat once more.
Add 500 µl wash buffer, close the lid, invert sharply 3 - 5 times, and centrifuge the column at 1000 g for 30 - 60 seconds.

Place the column into a fresh collection tube. Add up to 500 µl cleared lysate, close the lid, and incubate 5 minutes with end-over-end mixing. Centrifuge the column at 1000 g for 30 - 60 seconds. Repeat the loading step when your sample volume is larger than 500 µl. This is particularly recommended when your target protein has low expression levels. It is advisable to keep the flow-through and wash fractions for later analysis, e.g. SDS-PAGE.

Place the column into a fresh collection tube. Add 500 µl wash buffer, close the column, and invert sharply 3 - 5 times. Centrifuge the column at 1000 g for 30 - 60 seconds and empty the collection tube. Repeat once more without changing the collection tube. For increased purity, repeat the washing step up to 5 times.

Place the column into a fresh collection tube. Add 200 µl elution buffer to the column. Centrifuge the column at 1000 g for 30 - 60 seconds. Without changing the collection tube, repeat this elution step two more times.

Determine the weight of the frozen bacterial pellet and thaw it on ice. Resuspend the pellet in wash buffer by pipetting. For every gram of pellet add 3 - 5 milliliters buffer. Add lysozyme and DNaseI (not included in the kit) 0.01 Mass / % volume, 0.1mg/ml each Alternatively use ultrasonication according to the instructions of your instrument manufacturer. Pre-chill an appropriate centrifuge to 4 °C.

Incubate for 30 minutes with gentle end-over-end mixing, stirring, or rocking at room temperature. If your target protein is known to undergo proteolytic degradation incubate at 4 - 8 °C for 60 minutes. 22 °C 00:30:00 or 4 °C 01:00:00

Centrifuge the lysate for 30 minutes at >10.000 g at 4 °C . Carefully transfer the supernatant to a fresh tube. During centrifugation you can already proceed with the equilibration of the spin column.

Determine the weight of the frozen bacterial pellet and thaw it at room temperature. Resuspend the pellet in wash buffer by pipetting. For every gram of pellet add 3 - 5 milliliters buffer. Add lysozyme and DNaseI (not included in the kit) 0.01 Mass / % volume, 0.1mg/ml each Alternatively, use ultrasonication according to the instuctions of your instrument manufacturer and skip the next step. Pre-chill an appropriate centrifuge to 4 °C.

Incubate for 15 minutes with gentle end-over-end mixing, stirring, or rocking at room temperature. If your target protein is known to undergo proteolytic degradation or rapid denaturation, incubate at 4 - 8 °C for 30 minutes. 22 °C 00:15:00 or 4 °C 00:30:00

Centrifuge the lysate for 30 minutes at >10.000 g at 4 °C . Carefully transfer the supernatant to a fresh tube. During centrifugation you can already proceed with the equilibration of the spin column.

Deionized water Cleaning solution 1: 6 M Guanidine chloride, 0.2 M Acetic acid Cleaning solution 2: 1 % Sodium dodecyl sulfate (SDS) Cleaning solution 3: > 95 % Ethanol Cleaning solution 4: 0.1 M Ethylenediaminetetraacetic acid (EDTA) Cleaning solution 5: 0.2 M Acetic Acid Metal solution: 0.1 M Nickel(II) sulfate (or other metal ion of your choice) 20 % (v/v) Ethanol
Remove the bottom cap and drain any storage solution by centrifugation at 1000 g for 1 minute.

Use 500 µl of each cleaning solution, one after another, with two 500 µl washes with deionized water in between each of the cleaning solutions, i.e.: Add 500 µl of cleaning solution 1, invert sharply 4-5 times, centrifuge 1 minute at 1000 g, discard flow through. Add 500 µl of deionized water, invert sharply 4-5 times, centrifuge 1 minute at 1000 g, discard flow through. Add 500 µl of deionized water, invert sharply 4-5 times, centrifuge 1 minute at 1000 g, discard flow through. Add 500 µl of cleaning solution 2, invert sharply 4-5 times, centrifuge 1 minute at 1000 g, discard flow through. Add 500 µl of deionized water, invert sharply 4-5 times, centrifuge 1 minute at 1000 g, discard flow through. ...

Wash the column three times with deionized water as in the previous step. Add 500 µl of the Metal solution and incubate for 5 minutes with end-over-end mixing. Centrifuge 1 minute at 1000 g, discard the flow through. Wash two more times with deionized water.

Close the bottom of the column, add 500 µl 20 % Ethanol or wash buffer (contains 0.05 % (w/v) sodium azide), and close the lid and store at 4 - 8 °C Alternative for long-term storage: Dry the open (top and bottom) column in an oven at 80 °C for at least 2 hours or over night. Make sure the bottom stopper is completely dry, too. Put on the column's outlet, close the lid and store the column closed at room temperature.

After purification: wash the column with 500 µl elution buffer, centrifuge the column for 30 - 60 seconds at 1000 g, repeat with 500 µl wash buffer. If the column was stored before: remove the bottom cap and centrifuge the column for 30 - 60 seconds at 1000 g. Then add 500 µl of wash buffer and centrifuge the column for 30 - 60 seconds at 1000 g.
Close the bottom of the column and add 500 µl sanitization solution. Close the lid and incubate for one hour at RT.
Remove the bottom cap and centrifuge the column for 30 - 60 seconds at 1000 g. Wash three times with 500 µl of deionized water.
Close the bottom of the column, add 500 µl 20 % ethanol or wash buffer (contains 0.05 % (w/v) sodium azide). Close the lid and store at room temperature or at 4 - 8 °C.

Wash the column by adding 500 µl elution buffer and centrifuge at 1000 g for 30 - 60 seconds. Repeat this step with 500 µl wash buffer and 500 µl deionized water. Then, close the bottom of the column, add 500 µl 20 % ethanol or wash buffer (contains 0.05 % (w/v) sodium azide). Close the lid and store at room temperature or at 4 - 8 °C.

Wash the column by adding 500 µl elution buffer and centrifuge at 1000 g for 30 - 60 seconds. Repeat this step with 500 µl wash buffer and 500 µl deionized water. Then, close the bottom of the column, add 500 µl 20 % ethanol or wash buffer supplied in the kit (contains 0.05 % (w/v) sodium azide). Close the lid and store at room temperature or at 4 - 8 °C.
